** [[Australia]]n researchers say they are a step closer to finding a [[vaccine]] for [[HIV]], and hope to be able to offer a preventative jab by 2020.<ref>{{cite web | last=Hagan | first=Kate | title=Fresh hope in the fight against HIV | website=The Sydney Morning Herald | date=2011-05-06 | url=https://www.smh.com.au/world/fresh-hope-in-the-fight-against-hiv-20110506-1ec2s.html | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Chung | first1=Amy W. | last2=Isitman | first2=Gamze | last3=Navis | first3=Marjon | last4=Kramski | first4=Marit | last5=Center | first5=Rob J. | last6=Kent | first6=Stephen J. | last7=Stratov | first7=Ivan |display-authors=3| title=Immune escape from HIV-specific antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) pressure | journal=Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences | volume=108 | issue=18 | date=2011-05-03 | issn=0027-8424 | pmid=21502492 | pmc=3088575 | doi=10.1073/pnas.1016048108 | doi-access=free | pages=7505–7510| bibcode=2011PNAS..108.7505C }}</ref>
* 4 May
** [[Yukon]] fossils may represent the first early traces of [[biomineralization]] in [[eukaryote]]s.<ref>{{cite web | last=Keim | first=Brandon | title=Ancient Fossils Have Evolution's First Shells | website=wired.com | date=2011-06-13 | url=https://www.wired.com/wiredscience/2011/06/first-shells/ | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110615011132/https://www.wired.com/wiredscience/2011/06/first-shells/ | archive-date=2011-06-15 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Cohen | first1=Phoebe A. | last2=Schopf | first2=J. William | last3=Butterfield | first3=Nicholas J. | last4=Kudryavtsev | first4=Anatoliy B. | last5=Macdonald | first5=Francis A. | title=Phosphate biomineralization in mid-Neoproterozoic protists | journal=Geology | volume=39 | issue=6 | date=2011 | issn=1943-2682 | doi=10.1130/G31833.1 | pages=539–542| bibcode=2011Geo....39..539C | url=http://nrs.harvard.edu/urn-3:HUL.InstRepos:33717512 }}</ref>
** Experimental data gathered by the [[Gravity Probe B]] satellite [[Tests of general relativity#Frame-dragging tests|confirm]]s two aspects of the [[general theory of relativity]], which was published by [[Albert Einstein]] in 1916.<ref>{{cite web | last=Amos | first=Jonathan | title=Gravity Probe B confirms Einstein effects | website=BBC News | date=2011-05-04 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13286241 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Everitt | first1=C. W. F. | last2=DeBra | first2=D. B. | last3=Parkinson | first3=B. W. | last4=Turneaure | first4=J. P. | last5=Conklin | first5=J. W. | last6=Heifetz | first6=M. I. | last7=Keiser | first7=G. M. | last8=Silbergleit | first8=A. S. | last9=Holmes | first9=T. | last10=Kolodziejczak | first10=J. | display-authors=3| title=Gravity Probe B: Final Results of a Space Experiment to Test General Relativity | journal=Physical Review Letters | volume=106 | issue=22 | date=2011-05-31 | issn=0031-9007 | doi=10.1103/PhysRevLett.106.221101 | doi-access=free | article-number=221101| pmid=21702590 |arxiv=1105.3456| bibcode=2011PhRvL.106v1101E }}</ref>
** [[CERN]] scientists have confined [[antihydrogen]] atoms for 1,000 seconds, four orders of magnitude longer than has ever been achieved before in capturing and maintaining [[antimatter]] atoms.<ref>{{cite journal | author=The ALPHA Collaboration | title=Confinement of antihydrogen for 1,000 seconds | journal=Nature Physics | volume=7 | issue=7 | date=2011 | issn=1745-2473 | doi=10.1038/nphys2025 | doi-access=free | pages=558–564 |arxiv=1104.4982}}</ref>
** [[Intel]] unveils its next generation of [[microprocessor]] technology, codenamed [[Sandy Bridge#Ivy Bridge|Ivy Bridge]]. The upcoming chips will be the first to use a [[22 nanometer|22 nanometre manufacturing process]], which packs transistors more densely than the current [[32nm]] system, providing greater efficiency.<ref>{{cite web | title=Intel unveils 22nm 3D Ivy Bridge processor | website=BBC News | date=2011-05-04 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/technology-13283882 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | title=Intel Newsroom: News Stories: Intel Reinvents Transistors Using New 3-D Structure | website=newsroom.intel.com | date=2011-05-04 | url=http://newsroom.intel.com/community/intel_newsroom/blog/2011/05/04/intel-reinvents-transistors-using-new-3-d-structure | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110507231225/http://newsroom.intel.com/community/intel_newsroom/blog/2011/05/04/intel-reinvents-transistors-using-new-3-d-structure | archive-date=2011-05-07 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
* 6 May
** A new study suggests that the drop in production of [[neurons]] in [[old age]] is due to the shrinking cache of adult [[stem cell]]s in our brains.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/life-style/health-fitness/health/Why-our-brains-gradually-lose-memory-with-age/articleshow/8186879.cms|title=Why Our Brains Gradually Lose Memory with Age|website=Times of India}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Encinas | first1=Juan M. | last2=Michurina | first2=Tatyana V. | last3=Peunova | first3=Natalia | last4=Park | first4=June-Hee | last5=Tordo | first5=Julie | last6=Peterson | first6=Daniel A. | last7=Fishell | first7=Gord | last8=Koulakov | first8=Alex | last9=Enikolopov | first9=Grigori |display-authors=3 | title=Division-Coupled Astrocytic Differentiation and Age-Related Depletion of Neural Stem Cells in the Adult Hippocampus | journal=Cell Stem Cell | volume=8 | issue=5 | date=2011 | pmid=21549330 | pmc=3286186 | doi=10.1016/j.stem.2011.03.010 | doi-access=free | pages=566–579 | url=http://www.cell.com/article/S1934590911001202/pdf | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
** A machine used for measuring impurities in [[semiconductors]] can be used to analyze [[immune cells]] in far more detail than has been previously possible, researchers from [[Stanford University]] have shown.<ref>{{cite web | title=Revolutionary new single-cell labelling method | website=www.rsc.org | url=http://www.rsc.org/chemistryworld/News/2011/May/05051102.asp | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Bendall | first1=Sean C. | last2=Simonds | first2=Erin F. | last3=Qiu | first3=Peng | last4=Amir | first4=El-ad D. | last5=Krutzik | first5=Peter O. | last6=Finck | first6=Rachel | last7=Bruggner | first7=Robert V. | last8=Melamed | first8=Rachel | last9=Trejo | first9=Angelica | last10=Ornatsky | first10=Olga I. | last11=Balderas | first11=Robert S. | last12=Plevritis | first12=Sylvia K. | last13=Sachs | first13=Karen | last14=Pe'er | first14=Dana | last15=Tanner | first15=Scott D. | last16=Nolan | first16=Garry P. | display-authors=3|title=Single-Cell Mass Cytometry of Differential Immune and Drug Responses Across a Human Hematopoietic Continuum | journal=Science | volume=332 | issue=6030 | date=2011-05-06 | issn=0036-8075 | pmid=21551058 | pmc=3273988 | doi=10.1126/science.1198704 | doi-access=free | pages=687–696 | bibcode=2011Sci...332..687B }}</ref>
** Researchers have identified a group of [[mitochondrial]] [[protein]]s, the absence of which allows other protein groups to stabilise the [[genome]]. This could delay the onset of age-related diseases and increase lifespan.<ref>{{cite web | title=Mitochondrial proteins regulating human ageing 'identified' | website=The Economic Times | date=2011-05-11 | url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/news-by-industry/et-cetera/mitochondrial-proteins-regulating-human-ageing-identified/articleshow/8242110.cms | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Caballero | first1=Antonio | last2=Ugidos | first2=Ana | last3=Liu | first3=Beidong | last4=Öling | first4=David | last5=Kvint | first5=Kristian | last6=Hao | first6=Xinxin | last7=Mignat | first7=Cora | last8=Nachin | first8=Laurence | last9=Molin | first9=Mikael | last10=Nyström | first10=Thomas | display-authors=3|title=Absence of Mitochondrial Translation Control Proteins Extends Life Span by Activating Sirtuin-Dependent Silencing | journal=Molecular Cell | volume=42 | issue=3 | date=2011 | doi=10.1016/j.molcel.2011.03.021 | doi-access=free | pages=390–400 | pmid=21549315 | url=http://www.cell.com/article/S109727651100253X/pdf | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
* 9 May – [[Smog]]-eating [[aluminium]] panels which clean themselves and the air around them are unveiled; their [[titanium dioxide]] coating, when combined with sunlight, acts as a catalyst to break down pollutants into harmless matter that [[rain]] washes away.<ref>{{cite web | title=Newsroom | website=news.alcoa.com | url=http://www.alcoa.com/global/en/news/news_detail.asp?newsYear=2011&pageID=20110509005969en | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | last=Koch | first=Wendy | title=Smog-eating aluminum panels launch for buildings | website=usatoday.com | date=2011-05-13 | url=https://www.usatoday.com/money/industries/environment/2011-05-12-smog-eating-aluminum_n.htm | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110516072616/https://www.usatoday.com/money/industries/environment/2011-05-12-smog-eating-aluminum_n.htm | archive-date=2011-05-16 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | last=Woody | first=Todd | title=Alcoa's Self-Cleaning, Smog-Eating Buildings | website=Forbes | date=2011-05-09 | url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/toddwoody/2011/05/09/alcoas-self-cleaning-smog-eating-buildings/ | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
* 11 May
** A new [[phylum]] of [[fungi]] is announced, and named [[cryptomycota]] ("hidden fungi").<ref>{{cite web | last=Ghosh | first=Pallab | title='Missing link' fungi found in Devon pond | website=BBC News | date=2011-05-11 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13363932 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Jones | first1=Meredith D. M. | last2=Forn | first2=Irene | last3=Gadelha | first3=Catarina | last4=Egan | first4=Martin J. | last5=Bass | first5=David | last6=Massana | first6=Ramon | last7=Richards | first7=Thomas A. | title=Discovery of novel intermediate forms redefines the fungal tree of life | journal=Nature | volume=474 | issue=7350 | date=2011 | issn=0028-0836 | doi=10.1038/nature09984 | pages=200–203| pmid=21562490 }}</ref>
** A new vaccine can protect [[macaques]] against the monkey equivalent of [[HIV]], and could provide a fresh approach to an HIV vaccine, a study suggests.<ref>{{cite web | last=Bowdler | first=Neil | title=Monkey HIV vaccine 'effective', say researchers | website=BBC News | date=2011-05-11 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/health-13362927 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Hansen | first1=Scott G. | last2=Ford | first2=Julia C. | last3=Lewis | first3=Matthew S. | last4=Ventura | first4=Abigail B. | last5=Hughes | first5=Colette M. | last6=Coyne-Johnson | first6=Lia | last7=Whizin | first7=Nathan | last8=Oswald | first8=Kelli | last9=Shoemaker | first9=Rebecca | last10=Swanson | first10=Tonya | last11=Legasse | first11=Alfred W. | last12=Chiuchiolo | first12=Maria J. | last13=Parks | first13=Christopher L. | last14=Axthelm | first14=Michael K. | last15=Nelson | first15=Jay A. | last16=Jarvis | first16=Michael A. | last17=Piatak | first17=Michael | last18=Lifson | first18=Jeffrey D. | last19=Picker | first19=Louis J. |display-authors=3| title=Profound early control of highly pathogenic SIV by an effector memory T-cell vaccine | journal=Nature | volume=473 | issue=7348 | date=2011 | issn=0028-0836 | pmid=21562493 | pmc=3102768 | doi=10.1038/nature10003 | doi-access=free | pages=523–527 | bibcode=2011Natur.473..523H | url=https://www.nature.com/articles/nature10003.pdf | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
** [[D-Wave Systems]], after some 12 years of research, the accumulation of 60 patents, and the filing of 100 more, has released the world's first commercial [[quantum computer]], priced at $10 million.<ref>{{cite web | last=Knapp | first=Alex | title=D-Wave Announces Commercially Available Quantum Computer | website=Forbes | date=2011-05-17 | url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/alexknapp/2011/05/17/d-wave-announces-commerically-available-quantum-computer/ | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | last=Anthony | first=Sebastian | title=First Ever Commercial Quantum Computer Now Available for $10 Million | website=extremetech.com | date=1994-12-01 | url=http://www.extremetech.com/article2/0,2845,2385665,00.asp | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110605205041/http://www.extremetech.com/article2/0,2845,2385665,00.asp | archive-date=2011-06-05 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | author=Geordie &raquo | title=Learning to program the D-Wave One « Hack the multiverse | website=dwave.wordpress.com | date=2011-05-11 | url=http://dwave.wordpress.com/2011/05/11/learning-to-program-the-d-wave-one/ | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110516014127/http://dwave.wordpress.com/2011/05/11/learning-to-program-the-d-wave-one/ | archive-date=2011-05-16 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
* 12 May – The [[exoplanet]] [[Gliese 581d]] can be considered the first confirmed exoplanet that could potentially support Earth-like life, according to a team of French scientists.<ref>{{cite journal | last1=Wordsworth | first1=Robin D. | last2=Forget | first2=François | last3=Selsis | first3=Franck | last4=Millour | first4=Ehouarn | last5=Charnay | first5=Benjamin | last6=Madeleine | first6=Jean-Baptiste |display-authors=3| title=Gliese 581D is the First Discovered Terrestrial-Mass Exoplanet in the Habitable Zone | journal=The Astrophysical Journal | volume=733 | issue=2 | date=2011-06-01 | issn=2041-8205 | doi=10.1088/2041-8205/733/2/L48 | doi-access=free | page=L48 | arxiv=1105.1031 | bibcode=2011ApJ...733L..48W }}</ref>
[[File:Planet Gliese 581 d.png|thumb|right|12 May 2011: astronomers state that the [[exoplanet]] [[Gliese 581d]] (artist's impression pictured) could potentially support Earth-like life.]]
* 13 May
** New results from [[mice]] cast doubts on hopes for self-[[organ transplant|transplants]] generated from the receiver's [[stem cell]]s, as such transplants can be rejected by the immune system.<ref>{{cite web | last=Coghlan | first=Andy | title=Stem cell setback as mice reject own tissue | website=New Scientist | date=2011-05-13 | url=https://www.newscientist.com/article/dn20476-stem-cell-setback-as-mice-reject-own-tissue/ | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Zhao | first1=Tongbiao | last2=Zhang | first2=Zhen-Ning | last3=Rong | first3=Zhili | last4=Xu | first4=Yang | title=Immunogenicity of induced pluripotent stem cells | journal=Nature | volume=474 | issue=7350 | date=2011 | issn=0028-0836 | doi=10.1038/nature10135 | doi-access=free | pages=212–215 | pmid=21572395 | url=http://circres.ahajournals.org/content/109/7/720.full.pdf | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
** According to new research, a small set of genes located within the [[mitochondria]] of cells is crucial to unravelling the secrets of [[male infertility]].<ref>{{cite web | last=University | first=Monash | title=Research breakthrough on male infertility | website=Medical Xpress | date=2011-05-13 | url=https://medicalxpress.com/news/2011-05-breakthrough-male-infertility.html | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Innocenti | first1=Paolo | last2=Morrow | first2=Edward H. | last3=Dowling | first3=Damian K. | title=Experimental Evidence Supports a Sex-Specific Selective Sieve in Mitochondrial Genome Evolution | journal=Science | volume=332 | issue=6031 | date=2011-05-13 | issn=0036-8075 | doi=10.1126/science.1201157 | pages=845–848| pmid=21566193 | bibcode=2011Sci...332..845I }}</ref>
** The discovery of a new physical phenomenon could yield [[transistor]]s with greatly enhanced [[capacitance]] – a measure of the voltage required to move a charge. This, in turn, could lead to the revival of [[clock speed]] as the measure of a computer's power.<ref>{{cite journal | last1=Li | first1=Lu | last2=Richter | first2=C. | last3=Paetel | first3=S. | last4=Kopp | first4=T. | last5=Mannhart | first5=J. | last6=Ashoori | first6=R. C. | title=Very Large Capacitance Enhancement in a Two-Dimensional Electron System | journal=Science | volume=332 | issue=6031 | date=2011-05-13 | issn=0036-8075 | doi=10.1126/science.1204168 | doi-access=free | pages=825–828 | pmid=21566188 | arxiv=1006.2847 | bibcode=2011Sci...332..825L }}</ref>
** Contaminated water can be cleaned much more effectively using a novel, cheap material, which could offer a low-cost way to purify water in the developing world.<ref>{{cite web | last=Moskvitch | first=Katia | title='Super sand' to help clean up dirty drinking water | website=BBC News | date=2011-06-24 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/business-13895077 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Gao | first1=Wei | last2=Majumder | first2=Mainak | last3=Alemany | first3=Lawrence B. | last4=Narayanan | first4=Tharangattu N. | last5=Ibarra | first5=Miguel A. | last6=Pradhan | first6=Bhabendra K. | last7=Ajayan | first7=Pulickel M. |display-authors=3| title=Engineered Graphite Oxide Materials for Application in Water Purification | journal=ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces | volume=3 | issue=6 | date=2011-06-22 | issn=1944-8244 | doi=10.1021/am200300u | pages=1821–1826| pmid=21568266 | bibcode=2011AAMI....3.1821G }}</ref>
* 15 May – Researchers have found that [[KLF14]], a gene linked to [[type 2 diabetes]] and [[cholesterol]] levels, is in fact a 'master regulator' gene, which controls the behaviour of other genes found within [[adipose tissue|fat]] in the body.<ref>{{cite web | title=Researchers discover 'master switch' gene for obesity, diabetes | website=Medical Xpress | date=2011-05-15 | url=https://medicalxpress.com/news/2011-05-master-gene-obesity-diabetes.html | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | author=the MuTHER Consortium | title=Identification of an imprinted master trans regulator at the KLF14 locus related to multiple metabolic phenotypes | journal=Nature Genetics | volume=43 | issue=6 | date=2011 | issn=1061-4036 | pmid=21572415 | pmc=3192952 | doi=10.1038/ng.833 | doi-access=free | pages=561–564 }}</ref>
* 16 May – [[NASA]]'s [[Space Shuttle Endeavour|Space Shuttle ''Endeavour'']] launches on its final mission.<ref>{{cite web | last=Amos | first=Jonathan | title=Shuttle Endeavour launches one last time | website=BBC News | date=2011-05-15 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13394383 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
* 18 May
** Scientists have achieved [[invisibility]] in the visible light range of the [[spectrum]].<ref>{{cite journal | last1=Fischer | first1=Joachim | last2=Ergin | first2=Tolga | last3=Wegener | first3=Martin | title=Three-dimensional polarization-independent visible-frequency carpet invisibility cloak | journal=Optics Letters | volume=36 | issue=11 | date=2011-06-01 | pages=2059–2061 | issn=0146-9592 | doi=10.1364/OL.36.002059 | doi-access=free | pmid=21633448 | bibcode=2011OptL...36.2059F | url=http://esperia.iesl.forth.gr/%7Eppm/PHOME/publications/OL_36_2059_2011.pdf | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
** [[Rogue planet]]s lacking parent stars may outnumber "normal" [[exoplanets]] by at least 50 percent, and are nearly twice as common in our galaxy as main-sequence stars, according to a new study.<ref>{{cite web | last=Wall | first=Mike | title=Lonely Rogue Worlds Surprisingly Outnumber Planets with Suns | website=Space.com | date=2011-05-18 | url=https://www.space.com/11699-rogue-alien-planets-milky-common.html | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | author=The Microlensing Observations in Astrophysics (MOA) Collaboration | author2=The Optical Gravitational Lensing Experiment (OGLE) Collaboration | title=Unbound or distant planetary mass population detected by gravitational microlensing | journal=Nature | volume=473 | issue=7347 | date=2011-05-19 | issn=0028-0836 | doi=10.1038/nature10092 | doi-access=free | pages=349–352 | pmid=21593867 | arxiv=1105.3544 | bibcode=2011Natur.473..349S }}</ref>
* 19 May
** By using electrical stimulation of the [[spinal cord]], a man from [[Oregon]] who became paralyzed after being hit by a car can stand and move his legs on his own.<ref>{{cite web | last=Gallagher | first=James | title=Paralysed man can stand and move his legs again | website=BBC News | date=2011-05-19 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/health-13444036 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Harkema | first1=Susan | last2=Gerasimenko | first2=Yury | last3=Hodes | first3=Jonathan | last4=Burdick | first4=Joel | last5=Angeli | first5=Claudia | last6=Chen | first6=Yangsheng | last7=Ferreira | first7=Christie | last8=Willhite | first8=Andrea | last9=Rejc | first9=Enrico | last10=Grossman | first10=Robert G | last11=Edgerton | first11=V Reggie |display-authors=3| title=Effect of epidural stimulation of the lumbosacral spinal cord on voluntary movement, standing, and assisted stepping after motor complete paraplegia: a case study | journal=The Lancet | volume=377 | issue=9781 | date=2011 | pmid=21601270 | pmc=3154251 | doi=10.1016/S0140-6736(11)60547-3 | doi-access=free | pages=1938–1947 }}</ref>
** Scientists have developed an [[open-source]] desktop [[genome]] analyzer. It works in conjunction with a browser that allows biologists to rapidly and easily analyze and process their high-throughput information.<ref>{{cite web | last=Nova | first=Chris | title=Scientists develop open-source genome analyzer | website=tgdaily.com | date=2011-06-09 | url=http://www.tgdaily.com/trendwatch-brief/56514-scientists-develop-open-source-genome-analyzer | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110611171143/http://www.tgdaily.com/trendwatch-brief/56514-scientists-develop-open-source-genome-analyzer | archive-date=2011-06-11 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Lajugie | first1=Julien | last2=Bouhassira | first2=Eric E. | title=GenPlay, a multipurpose genome analyzer and browser | journal=Bioinformatics | volume=27 | issue=14 | date=2011-07-15 | issn=1367-4803 | doi=10.1093/bioinformatics/btr309 | doi-access=free | pages=1889–1893 | pmid=21596789 | url=https://academic.oup.com/bioinformatics/article-pdf/27/14/1889/48933277/bioinformatics_27_14_1889.pdf | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
* 20 May – A highly developed [[sense of smell]] kick-started the evolution of mammals' big brains, according to new research.<ref>{{cite web | last=Gill | first=Victoria | title=Smell key to big brain evolution | website=bbc.co.uk | date=2011-05-20 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/nature/13448202 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110522132645/https://www.bbc.co.uk/nature/13448202 | archive-date=2011-05-22 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last=Callaway | first=Ewen | title=Mammalian brain followed a scented evolutionary trail | journal=Nature | date=2011-05-19 | issn=0028-0836 | doi=10.1038/news.2011.302 | page=}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Rowe | first1=Timothy B. | last2=Macrini | first2=Thomas E. | last3=Luo | first3=Zhe-Xi | title=Fossil Evidence on Origin of the Mammalian Brain | journal=Science | volume=332 | issue=6032 | date=2011-05-20 | issn=0036-8075 | doi=10.1126/science.1203117 | pages=955–957| pmid=21596988 | bibcode=2011Sci...332..955R }}</ref>
* 23 May
** Researchers have set a new record for the rate of data transfer using a single [[laser]]: 26 [[terabit]]s per second.<ref>{{cite web | last=Palmer | first=Jason | title=Laser puts record data rate through fibre | website=BBC News | date=2011-05-22 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13469924 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Hillerkuss | first1=D. | last2=Schmogrow | first2=R. | last3=Schellinger | first3=T. | last4=Jordan | first4=M. | last5=Winter | first5=M. | last6=Huber | first6=G. | last7=Vallaitis | first7=T. | last8=Bonk | first8=R. | last9=Kleinow | first9=P. | last10=Frey | first10=F. | display-authors=3 | title=26 Tbit s−1 line-rate super-channel transmission utilizing all-optical fast Fourier transform processing | journal=Nature Photonics | volume=5 | issue=6 | date=2011 | issn=1749-4885 | doi=10.1038/nphoton.2011.74 | pages=364–371}}</ref>
** The bacteria responsible for [[stomach ulcers]] have been linked to [[Parkinson's disease]], according to American researchers.<ref>{{cite web | title=Bacteria 'linked' to Parkinson's disease | website=BBC News | date=2011-05-22 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/health-13469716 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | last=Sliwa | first=Jim | title=Bacteria may cause Parkinson's disease | website=gm.asm.org | date=2011-07-22 | url=http://gm.asm.org/index.php/component/content/article/48/168-bacteria-may-cause-parkinsons-disease | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110613014627/http://gm.asm.org/index.php/component/content/article/48/168-bacteria-may-cause-parkinsons-disease | archive-date=2011-06-13 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
* 24 May – A superhot substance recently made in the [[Large Hadron Collider]] is the densest form of matter ever observed, scientists have announced.<ref>{{cite web | title=Densest Matter Created in Big-Bang Machine | website=news.nationalgeographic.com | date=2011-05-11 | url=http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2011/05/110524-densest-matter-created-lhc-alice-big-bang-space-science/ | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110525231728/http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2011/05/110524-densest-matter-created-lhc-alice-big-bang-space-science/ | archive-date=2011-05-25 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
[[File:Gyrus Dentatus 40x.jpg|thumb|right|26 May 2011: American scientists successfully transmute human skin cells directly into [[neurons]] (pictured).]]
* 25 May
** Reexamination of data indicates that the [[gamma-ray burst]] [[GRB 090423]] may be the most distant single object yet detected; scientists believe the blast, which was detected by NASA's [[Swift Observatory]], occurred a mere 520 million years after the [[Big Bang]].<ref>{{cite web | last=Amos | first=Jonathan | title=Cosmic distance record 'broken' | website=BBC News | date=2011-05-25 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13539914 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Cucchiara | first1=A. | last2=Levan | first2=A. J. | last3=Fox | first3=D. B. | last4=Tanvir | first4=N. R. | last5=Ukwatta | first5=T. N. | last6=Berger | first6=E. | last7=Krühler | first7=T. | last8=Yoldaş | first8=A. Küpcü | last9=Wu | first9=X. F. | last10=Toma | first10=K. | display-authors=3| title=A PHOTOMETRIC REDSHIFT OF z ∼ 9.4 FOR GRB 090429B | journal=The Astrophysical Journal | volume=736 | issue=1 | date=2011-07-20 | issn=0004-637X | doi=10.1088/0004-637X/736/1/7 | doi-access=free | page=7 | arxiv=1105.4915| bibcode=2011ApJ...736....7C }}</ref>
** NASA ends its operational planning activities for the veteran [[Mars rover]] ''[[Spirit rover|Spirit]]''; it will now transition the [[Mars Exploration Rover|Mars Exploration Rover Project]] to a single-rover operation focused on Spirit's still-active twin, ''[[Opportunity rover|Opportunity]]''.<ref>{{cite web | last=Khan | first=Amina | title=Spirit's Mars mission comes to a close | website=Los Angeles Times | date=2011-05-25 | url=https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-2011-may-25-la-sci-rover-spirit-20110525-story.html | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | last=Callas | first=John | title=NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory » Blog Archive » A Heartfelt Goodbye to a Spirited Mars Rover | website=blogs.jpl.nasa.gov | date=2011-07-15 | url=http://blogs.jpl.nasa.gov/2011/05/a-heartfelt-goodbye-to-a-spirited-mars-rover/ | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110528024734/http://blogs.jpl.nasa.gov/2011/05/a-heartfelt-goodbye-to-a-spirited-mars-rover/ | archive-date=2011-05-28 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
** Swedish scientists unveil a technique that causes the brain to misinterpret the size of the human body.<ref>{{cite web | last=Choi | first=Charles Q. | title=Scientists fool people into thinking they're tiny or giant | website=NBC News | date=2011-05-27 | url=https://www.nbcnews.com/id/wbna43196143 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=van der Hoort | first1=Björn | last2=Guterstam | first2=Arvid | last3=Ehrsson | first3=H. Henrik | title=Being Barbie: The Size of One's Own Body Determines the Perceived Size of the World | journal=PLOS ONE | volume=6 | issue=5 | date=2011-05-25 | issn=1932-6203 | pmid=21633503 | pmc=3102093 | doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0020195 | doi-access=free |article-number=e20195| bibcode=2011PLoSO...620195V }}</ref>
* 26 May
** Stanford University researchers have managed to turn human skin cells directly into [[neuron]]s, without first turning them into [[pluripotent stem cell]]s.<ref>{{cite web | title=Wayback Machine | website=Wayback Machine | date=2011-05-26 | url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110901000000*/http://blogs.discovermagazine.com/notrocketscience/2011/05/26/scientists-transform-skin-cells-directly-into-neurons/ | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Pang | first1=Zhiping P. | last2=Yang | first2=Nan | last3=Vierbuchen | first3=Thomas | last4=Ostermeier | first4=Austin | last5=Fuentes | first5=Daniel R. | last6=Yang | first6=Troy Q. | last7=Citri | first7=Ami | last8=Sebastiano | first8=Vittorio | last9=Marro | first9=Samuele | last10=Südhof | first10=Thomas C. | display-authors=3| title=Induction of human neuronal cells by defined transcription factors | journal=Nature | volume=476 | issue=7359 | date=2011 | issn=0028-0836 | pmid=21617644 | pmc=3159048 | doi=10.1038/nature10202 | doi-access=free | pages=220–223 | bibcode=2011Natur.476..220P }}</ref>
** Researchers believe they have made the first experimental observation of the dynamical [[Casimir effect]], using a rapidly moving mirror that turns virtual [[photon]]s into real ones.<ref>{{cite web | title=First Observation of the Dynamical Casimir Effect | website=technologyreview.com | date=2011-09-12 | url=http://www.technologyreview.com/blog/arxiv/26813/ | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110528155718/http://www.technologyreview.com/blog/arxiv/26813/ | archive-date=2011-05-28 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
* 29 May – Human organs could be grown inside [[pigs]] for use in transplant operations, following research using [[stem cell]]s.<ref>{{cite web | last=Gray | first=Richard | title=Pigs could grow human organs in stem cell breakthrough | website=telegraph.co.uk | date=2011-06-19 | url=http://www.telegraph.co.uk/science/science-news/8584443/Pigs-could-grow-human-organs-in-stem-cell-breakthrough.html | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110621231434/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/science/science-news/8584443/Pigs-could-grow-human-organs-in-stem-cell-breakthrough.html | archive-date=2011-06-21 | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | last=Design | first=ISITE | title=OASIS | website=CTI Meeting Technology | url=https://www.abstractsonline.com:443/Plan/ViewAbstract.aspx?sKey=e5e16659-ec14-4fbe-b697-1ec57c430230&cKey=b2d8bc27-be1d-4482-af44-4a2b115a116f&mKey=%7BDFC2C4B1-FBCD-433D-86DD-B15521A77070%7D | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>
* 31 May
** A team of [[China|Chinese]] physicists successfully [[quantum entanglement|entangles]] eight [[photon]]s simultaneously and observes them in action; the previous record was six.<ref>{{cite journal | last1=Yao | first1=Xing-Can | last2=Wang | first2=Tian-Xiong | last3=Xu | first3=Ping | last4=Lu | first4=He | last5=Pan | first5=Ge-Sheng | last6=Bao | first6=Xiao-Hui | last7=Peng | first7=Cheng-Zhi | last8=Lu | first8=Chao-Yang | last9=Chen | first9=Yu-Ao | last10=Pan | first10=Jian-Wei |display-authors=3| title=Observation of eight-photon entanglement | journal=Nature Photonics | volume=6 | issue=4 | date=2012 | issn=1749-4885 | doi=10.1038/nphoton.2011.354 | doi-access=free | pages=225–228 | arxiv=1105.6318| bibcode=2012NaPho...6..225Y }}</ref>
** Researchers have demonstrated the first true nanoscale waveguides for next generation on-chip optical communication systems; this holds potential for nanoscale photonic applications such as intra-chip optical communication, signal modulation, nanoscale lasers and bio-medical sensing.<ref>{{cite journal | last1=Sorger | first1=Volker J. | last2=Ye | first2=Ziliang | last3=Oulton | first3=Rupert F. | last4=Wang | first4=Yuan | last5=Bartal | first5=Guy | last6=Yin | first6=Xiaobo | last7=Zhang | first7=Xiang | title=Experimental demonstration of low-loss optical waveguiding at deep sub-wavelength scales | journal=Nature Communications | volume=2 | issue=1 | date=2011-05-31 | issn=2041-1723 | doi=10.1038/ncomms1315 | doi-access=free | url=https://www.nature.com/articles/ncomms1315.pdf | access-date=2025-01-04 | page=331| bibcode=2011NatCo...2..331S }}</ref>
** A NASA-led research team unveils the most precise map ever produced of the [[carbon]] stored in Earth's tropical forests; the data is expected to provide a baseline for ongoing carbon monitoring and research.<ref>{{cite journal | last1=Saatchi | first1=Sassan S. | last2=Harris | first2=Nancy L. | last3=Brown | first3=Sandra | last4=Lefsky | first4=Michael | last5=Mitchard | first5=Edward T. A. | last6=Salas | first6=William | last7=Zutta | first7=Brian R. | last8=Buermann | first8=Wolfgang | last9=Lewis | first9=Simon L. | last10=Hagen | first10=Stephen | display-authors=3| title=Benchmark map of forest carbon stocks in tropical regions across three continents | journal=Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences | volume=108 | issue=24 | date=2011-06-14 | issn=0027-8424 | pmid=21628575 | pmc=3116381 | doi=10.1073/pnas.1019576108 | doi-access=free | pages=9899–9904 | bibcode=2011PNAS..108.9899S | url=https://www.pnas.org/content/pnas/108/24/9899.full.pdf | access-date=2025-01-04}}</ref>

=== June ===
[[File:Simple Periodic Table Chart-en.svg|thumb|right|1 June 2011: two new [[chemical elements]], [[Element 114|114]] and [[Element 116|116]], are officially added to the [[periodic table]].]]
* June – [[Baltic Sea anomaly]] discovered.<ref>{{cite news|last1=Wolchover|first1=Natalie|title='Mysterious' Baltic Sea Object Is a Glacial Deposit|url=https://www.livescience.com/22846-mysterious-baltic-sea-object-is-a-glacial-deposit.html|newspaper=Livescience.com|date=2012-08-30|access-date=2020-01-25}}</ref>
* 1 June
** [[Element 114|Elements 114]] and [[Element 116|116]] are officially added to the [[periodic table]], becoming its heaviest members yet. [https://www.newscientist.com/article/dn20540-heaviest-elements-yet-join-periodic-table.html?DCMP=OTC-rss&nsref=online-news (''New Scientist'')] [https://web.archive.org/web/20110604155537/http://www.iupac.org/web/nt/2011-06-01_elements_114_116 (IUPAC)] {{doi-inline|10.1351/PAC-REP-10-05-01|(''Pure Appl. Chem.'')}}
** Scientists have discovered a worm that is the deepest-living animal known, surviving in 48-degree-[[Celsius]] (118 [[Fahrenheit]]) water at depths of {{convert|1.3|km|mi}}. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13620701 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1038/nature09974|(''Nature'')}}
* 2 June – A team of students at the [[University of California]] is developing a first-of-its kind, phase-change memory [[solid state drive|solid-state]] storage device that provides performance thousands of times faster than a conventional [[hard drive]], and up to seven times faster than current state-of-the-art solid-state drives. [http://www.jacobsschool.ucsd.edu/news/news_releases/release.sfe?id=1078 (Jacobs School of Engineering)]
* 3 June
** Researchers have bent one of the most basic rules of [[quantum mechanics]] by succeeding in observing light behaving as both a wave and a particle. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13626587 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1126/science.1202218|(''Science'')}}
** Six men in the [[MARS-500]] facility near Moscow have now been in isolation for exactly 365 days, simulating a human mission to [[Mars]]. [https://www.newscientist.com/blogs/shortsharpscience/2011/06/a-year-in-the-life-of-a-marson.html (''New Scientist'')] [http://www.esa.int/esaCP/SEMGI068BOG_index_0.html (ESA)]
** About one in 10 rocky planets around stars like the [[Sun]] may host a [[Natural satellite|moon]] proportionally as large [[Moon|as Earth's]]. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13609153 (BBC)] [https://arxiv.org/abs/1105.4616 (arXiv)] {{doi-inline|10.1016/j.icarus.2011.05.025|(''Icarus'')}}
* 6 June – A team of [[Virginia Commonwealth University]] scientists has discovered a new class of 'superatoms' – a stable cluster of [[atom]]s that can mimic different elements of the periodic table – with unusual magnetic characteristics.{{doi-inline|10.1073/pnas.1100129108|(''PNAS'')}}
* 7 June – Fragranced clothing, triggered by scent molecules that are stable in the dark and only release their aroma when exposed to light, has been described in a thesis written by scientist Dr. Olga Hinze of [[Cologne University]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20110615153600/http://www.henkel.com/SID-06BCB14D-04FF0F95/press/press-releases-2011-20110607-light-triggered-fragrance-32662.htm (Henkel)]
* 8 June – China's [[carbon dioxide]] emissions rose 10.4 percent in 2010 compared with the previous year, as global emissions rose at their fastest rate for more than four decades, according to data released by [[BP]]. [https://www.reuters.com/article/us-energy-bp-emissions-idUSTRE75728120110608 (Reuters)] [http://www.bp.com/sectionbodycopy.do?categoryId=7500&contentId=7068481 (BP)]
* 9 June
** Researchers have achieved a breakthrough in anti-[[bacteria]]l science, identifying natural ingredients capable of eradicating bacteria that have developed resistance to [[antibiotics]]. [http://www.tno.nl/content.cfm?context=overtno&content=persbericht&laag1=37&item_id=201106080029&Taal=2 (TNO)]
** [[Type 2 diabetes]], previously regarded as inevitably progressive, is successfully reversed in a group of newly diagnosed patients by an extreme eight-week diet of 600 calories a day. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/health-13887909 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1007/s00125-011-2204-7|(''Diabetologia'')}}
* 10 June
** Cross-checks on data that hinted at the discovery of a new [[sub-atomic particle]] have failed to find support for the observation. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13722986 (BBC)] [https://arxiv.org/abs/1106.1921 (arXiv)] {{doi-inline|10.1103/PhysRevLett.107.011804|''Phys. Rev. Lett.''}}
** US scientists publish data about how [[nicotine]] acts as an appetite suppressant, a finding that could help in fighting obesity. [https://www.smh.com.au/lifestyle/wellbeing/scientists-hit-on-why-smokers-are-thinner-20110610-1fvwu.html (''Sunday Morning Herald'')] {{doi-inline|10.1126/science.1201889|(''Science'')}}
[[File:Nabro Volcano ash cloud 2011-06-13, Eritrea.jpg|thumb|right|12 June 2011: the [[Nabro Volcano]] erupts in [[Eritrea]] (ash plume pictured), despite having been considered [[extinct volcano|extinct]].]]
* 12 June – The [[Nabro Volcano]] begins to [[2011 eruption of Nabro|erupt]], releasing the highest quantity of [[sulfur dioxide]] ever observed by satellite. {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/20110625040147/http://earthquake-report.com/2011/06/24/unusual-series-of-moderate-volcanic-earthquakes-in-eritrea-and-ethiopia/ (Earthquake-Report.com)]}}
* 13 June – A study suggests that [[protostars]] may be seeding the universe with water. These stellar embryos shoot jets of material from their north and south poles as their growth is fed by infalling dust, which circles the bodies in vast disks. [https://web.archive.org/web/20110616141849/http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2011/06/110613-space-science-star-water-bullets-kristensen (''National Geographic'')] {{doi-inline|10.1051/0004-6361/201116758|(''Astron. Astrophys.'')}}
* 14 June
** A study reveals that, on average, three to five days of global human activity produces the equivalent amount of carbon dioxide that volcanoes produce globally each year. [http://www.usgs.gov/newsroom/article.asp?ID=2827&from=rss_home (US Geological Survey)]
** Ten new planets outside our Solar System have been spotted by the French-led [[COROT]] satellite, bringing the total number of known [[exoplanets]] to 561. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13761405 (BBC)]
** A Japanese experiment sees hints that [[neutrino]] particles can oscillate between all three types, opening new lines of research to test why matter became more prevalent than [[antimatter]] in the [[Big Bang]]. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13763641 (BBC)] [https://arxiv.org/abs/1106.2822 (arXiv)] {{doi-inline|10.1103/PhysRevLett.107.041801|''Phys. Rev. Lett.''}}
* 15 June – A central [[June 2011 lunar eclipse|lunar eclipse]] takes place, with a totality of 1 hour and 40 minutes. (''[http://www.heraldsun.com.au/news/more-news/longest-lunar-eclipse-for-a-decade-for-south-australia-stargazers/story-fn7x8me2-1226075835237 Herald Sun]'')
* 16 June – Researchers have developed a scalable approach to fabricating high-speed [[graphene]] transistors. {{doi-inline|10.1021/nl201922c|''Nano Lett.''}}
* 17 June
** The [[United States Department of Energy]] reports that it will invest $150 million in a private company that has developed a [[silicon]]-wafer [[solar cell]] that can be manufactured twice as cheaply as standard solar cells. [https://www.huffingtonpost.com/2011/06/17/dept-of-energy-makes-150m_n_879542.html (''Huffington Post'')]
** Thousands of insects are being lined up to have their [[genome]]s sequenced. The five-year project will help researchers pinpoint vulnerable regions of insects' genomes, which could be targeted with [[pesticide]]s. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13778365 (BBC)] [http://www.entsoc.org/press-releases/entomologists-launch-5000-insect-genome-project-i5k (Entomological Society of America)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160329221712/http://entsoc.org/press-releases/entomologists-launch-5000-insect-genome-project-i5k |date=2016-03-29 }}
** Scientists have developed a nano-device that powers itself by harvesting energy from vibrations, while at the same time transmitting data wirelessly with a range of up to {{convert|10|m|ft}}. [http://www.popsci.com/science/article/2011-06/first-self-powering-nano-device-can-also-transmit-data-over-long-distances (''PopSci'')] {{doi-inline|10.1021/nl201505c|(''Nano Lett.'')}}
* 19 June
** Researchers have used a human vaccine to cure [[prostate cancer]] in mice. [http://medicalxpress.com/news/2011-06-approach-cancer-vaccines-successful-early.html (''Medical Xpress'')] {{doi-inline|10.1038/nm.2390|(''Nat. Med.'')}}
** The [[oceans]] are in a worse state than previously suspected, with a [[mass extinction]] of marine species looming, according to a new report. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13796479 (BBC)] [https://web.archive.org/web/20110626102633/http://www.stateoftheocean.org/pdfs/1906_IPSO-LONG.pdf (IPSO)]
* 20 June – A [[Japan]]ese computer has taken first place on the [http://www.top500.org/list/2011/06/100 Top 500 supercomputer list] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170701150947/https://www.top500.org/list/2011/06/100/ |date=2017-07-01 }}, ending [[China]]'s reign at the top after just six months. Capable of operating at 8.16 [[petaflops]] (quadrillion floating-point calculations per second), the [[K computer]] is more powerful than the next five systems combined. [http://www.computerworld.com/s/article/9217755/Japan_takes_back_first_place_on_Top_500_supercomputer_list?taxonomyId=12 (''ComputerWorld'')] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110623132519/http://www.computerworld.com/s/article/9217755/Japan_takes_back_first_place_on_Top_500_supercomputer_list?taxonomyId=12 |date=2011-06-23 }}
[[File:Epitaxial Nanowire Heterostructures SEM image.jpg|thumb|right|22 June 2011: [[Stanford University]] engineers develop [[nanowire]] electronics that can be attached to nearly any surface ([[gold]] nanowires pictured).]]
* 22 June
** A newly developed [[multiferroic]] composite of [[nickel]], [[cobalt]], [[manganese]] and [[tin]] can be either non-magnetic or highly magnetic, depending on its temperature, making it capable of converting heat into electricity. [http://www.popsci.com/technology/article/2011-06/new-alloy-can-convert-heat-directly-electricity (''PopSci'')] {{doi-inline|10.1002/aenm.201000048|(''Adv. Energy Mater.'')}}
** The brains of people living in cities operate differently from those in rural areas, according to a brain-scanning study. [https://www.theguardian.com/science/2011/jun/22/city-living-afffects-brain (''The Guardian'')] {{doi-inline|10.1038/nature10190|(''Nature'')}}
** Scientists demonstrate an acoustic "[[cloaking device]]" that makes objects invisible to [[sound waves]]; such acoustic cloaking was proposed theoretically in [[2008 in science|2008]], but has only this year been put into practice. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13905573 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1103/PhysRevLett.1|(''Phys. Rev. Lett.'')}}
** [[Stanford University]] researchers have developed a new method of attaching [[nanowire]] electronics to the surface of virtually any object, regardless of its shape or composition. The method could be used in making everything from wearable electronics and flexible computer displays to high-efficiency [[solar cell]]s and ultrasensitive biosensors.{{doi-inline|10.1021/nl201901z|(''Nano Lett.'')}}
* 23 June – Single-celled [[yeast]] has been observed to evolve into a multicellular organism, complete with division of labour between cells. This suggests that the evolutionary leap to multicellularity may be a surprisingly small hurdle.[https://www.newscientist.com/article/mg21028184.300-lab-yeast-make-evolutionary-leap-to-multicellularity.html (''New Scientist'')] {{doi-inline|10.1073/pnas.1115323109|(''PNAS'')}}
* 24 June
** A tiny biological [[fuel cell]] powered by [[bacteria]], with a capacity of just 0.3 [[microliter]]s, has been built by researchers at [[Carnegie Mellon University]] (CMU). The new device, the size of a single strand of human hair, generates energy from the metabolism of bacteria on thin [[gold]] plates in micro-manufactured channels. [https://www.engadget.com/2011/06/21/carnegie-mellon-researchers-develop-worlds-smallest-biological/ (''Engadget'')] {{doi-inline|10.1002/bit.23156| (''Biotechnol. Bioeng.'')}}
** Biologists publish the explanation for [[yeast]] cells reversing aging.  {{doi-inline|10.1126/science.1204349|(''Science'')}}
* 25 June – Stanford researchers have developed a microphone that can be used at any depth in the ocean, even under crushing pressure, and is sensitive to a wide range of sounds, from a whisper in a library to an explosion of [[Trinitrotoluene|TNT]]. They modeled their device after the extraordinarily acute hearing of [[orcas]]. {{doi-inline|10.1121/1.3543949|(''J. Acoust. Soc. Am.'')}}
26 June 
** A new gene-editing technique provides the first published successful healing of a genetic condition in a live animal, by curing mice of [[haemophilia B]]. [https://www.theguardian.com/science/2011/jun/26/doctors-breakthrough-repairing-genetic-defects (''The Guardian'')] {{doi-inline|10.1038/news.2011.384|(''Nature News'')}} {{doi-inline|10.1038/nature10177|(''Nature'')}}
** [[Österplana 065]] meteorite is found in Sweden. [http://www.lpi.usra.edu/meteor/metbull.php?code=63063 (Meteoritical Bulletin)]
* 27 June – A new bacterium is reported to have been produced from an engineered [[DNA]] sequence, in which [[thymine]] was replaced by the synthetic building block 5-chlorouracil – a substance "toxic to other organisms". {{doi-inline|10.1002/anie.201100535|(''Angew. Chem.'')}}
* 28 June – The United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization holds a ceremony in [[Rome]], declaring the once-widespread cattle disease [[rinderpest]] to be globally eradicated.[https://www.nytimes.com/2011/06/28/health/28rinderpest.html (''The New York Times'')] [http://www.fao.org/news/story/en/item/80728/icode/ (FAO)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111224210958/http://www.fao.org/news/story/en/item/80728/icode/ |date=2011-12-24 }}
* 30 June – Computer corporation [[IBM]] develops a form of 'instantaneous' memory, 100 times faster than [[flash memory]]. [https://www.engadget.com/2011/06/30/embargo-ibm-develops-instantaneous-memory-100x-faster-than-fl/ (Engadget)]

=== July ===
[[File:Illu conducting passages.svg|thumb|right|7 July 2011: Swedish surgeons successfully carry out the world's first artificial [[organ transplant]], giving a [[cancer]] patient a new, lab-grown [[Vertebrate trachea|trachea]].]]
* 1 July – Based on results from the [[Tevatron|Tevatron particle accelerator]], scientists have reported stronger evidence that a small excess of matter over [[antimatter]] was present during the [[Big Bang]] as particles decayed. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-13988836 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1103/PhysRevD.84.052007|(''Phys. Rev. D'')}}
* 3 July
** [[China]]'s monopoly over [[rare-earth metals]] could be challenged by the discovery of massive deposits of these widely used minerals on the floor of the [[Pacific Ocean]], a new study suggests. {{doi-inline|10.1038/ngeo1185|(''Nat. Geosci.'')}}
** Warming oceans will melt [[glacier]]s quicker than expected, according to a new study. As oceans heat up, they could erode ice sheets much faster than warmer air alone. [http://www.livescience.com/14892-warming-ocean-melting-ice-sheets.html (LiveScience)] {{doi-inline|10.1038/ngeo1189|(''Nat. Geosci.'')}}
* 7 July
** Surgeons in [[Sweden]] have carried out the world's first synthetic [[organ transplant]], using an artificial [[windpipe]] coated in [[stem cell]]s. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/health-14047670 (BBC)] [https://archive.today/20120903220512/http://www.karolinska.se/en/OM/press-nyheter/nyhetslista/First-Successful-Transplantation-of-a-Synthetic-Tissue-Engineered-Windpipe-/ (Karolinska Hospital)]
** Global investment in [[Renewable energy|renewable energy sources]] grew by 32% during 2010 to reach a record level of US$211 billion, according to a UN study. Reportedly, the main drivers of investment growth were [[wind farm]]s in China and rooftop [[solar panel]]s in Europe. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14030849 (BBC)] [https://web.archive.org/web/20110712131825/http://www.unep.org/Renewable_Energy_Investment/ (UNEP)]
** The molecular basis for the breakage of [[DNA]], an important process in the development of [[cancer]], has been identified by [[Hebrew University of Jerusalem]] scientists. [https://www.jpost.com/HealthAndSci-Tech/Health/Article.aspx?id=228363 (''The Jerusalem Post'')] {{doi-inline|10.1016/j.molcel.2011.05.019|(''Mol. Cell'')}}
* 9 July – Researchers have reprogrammed [[brain cells]] to become [[heart]] cells. {{doi-inline|10.1073/pnas.1101223108|(''PNAS'')}}
* 10 July – An international team of scientists based in [[Scotland]] have decoded the full [[DNA]] sequence of the [[potato]], one of the world's most important staple crops, for the first time. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14096485 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1038/nature10158|(''Nature'')}}
* 12 July
** A computer has learned language by playing [[strategy game]]s, inferring the meaning of words without human supervision. [http://web.mit.edu/newsoffice/2011/language-from-games-0712.html (MIT)] [https://web.archive.org/web/20110726175331/http://www.acl2011.org/accepted/627.html (ACL)]
** Researchers at the [[University of Cambridge]] have identified a part of the brain associated with [[empathy]] which may be a 'biomarker' for a familial risk of [[autism]]. {{doi-inline|10.1038/tp.2011.18|(''Transl. Psychiatry'')}}
** The planet [[Neptune]] completes its first orbit since it was discovered in [[1846 in science|1846]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.space.com/12249-neptune-completes-orbit-discovery-1846.html|title=Neptune Completes First Orbit Since Its Discovery in 1846|work=Space.com|date=12 July 2011}}</ref> 
[[File:Vesta from Dawn, July 17.jpg|thumb|right|16 July 2011: [[NASA]]'s ''[[Dawn (spacecraft)|Dawn]]'' spacecraft successfully enters orbit around the [[asteroid]] [[4 Vesta]] (pictured).]]
* 13 July
** A string of a dozen underwater [[volcanoes]], several of them active, has been found near [[Antarctica]], the first such discovery in that region. [http://ca.news.yahoo.com/huge-underwater-volcanoes-discovered-near-antarctica-185010146.html (Yahoo!)]
** Technicians from [[Kagawa University]] demonstrate a [[bionic]] [[mouth]] that replicates almost all the human organs that are required for singing. [https://spectrum.ieee.org/freaky-robot-mouth-learns-to-sing (IEEE Spectrum)]
* 14 July – A "fountain of youth" that sustains the production of new [[neuron]]s in the brains of rodents may also be present in the human brain, researchers have found.  {{doi-inline|10.1016/j.neuron.2011.05.029|(''Neuron'')}}
* 16 July
** [[NASA]]'s ''[[Dawn (spacecraft)|Dawn]]'' probe enters orbit around the asteroid [[4 Vesta]]. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14160135 (BBC)] [https://web.archive.org/web/20120322202418/http://www.jpl.nasa.gov/asteroidwatch/newsfeatures.cfm?release=2011-212 (NASA)]
** [[Japan]]ese company [[Sumitomo Electric Industries]] develops a new material which they believe can improve the range of [[electric vehicles]] by 300%. [http://inhabitat.com/aluminum-celmet-could-increase-electric-vehicle-range-by-300/ (Inhabitat)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110718095124/http://inhabitat.com/aluminum-celmet-could-increase-electric-vehicle-range-by-300 |date=2011-07-18 }} [https://web.archive.org/web/20120102021025/http://global-sei.com/news/press/11/11_19.html (Suitomo Electric)]
* 19 July
** [[Russia]]'s [[RadioAstron]], the largest orbital [[radio telescope]] yet constructed, is successfully launched into Earth orbit. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/technology-14200971 (BBC)]
** It is announced the [[Herschel Space Observatory]] has discovered a dense ribbon of gas and dust more than 600 light years across at the centre of the [[Milky Way]] galaxy. [http://herschel.cf.ac.uk/results/twisted-ring-galactic-centre (Herschel)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110724040240/http://herschel.cf.ac.uk/results/twisted-ring-galactic-centre |date=2011-07-24 }}
* 20 July
** The [[Hubble Space Telescope]] discovers [[Moons of Pluto|another moon]] orbiting [[Pluto]]. [http://www.cbat.eps.harvard.edu/cbet/cbet002769.txt (IAU)]
** An experiment at the [[Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory]] has revealed a heavy relative of the [[neutron]]. [http://www.livescience.com/15151-subatomic-particle.html (LiveScience)] [http://www.fnal.gov/pub/presspass/press_releases/2011/CDF-Xi-sub-b-observation-20110720.html (FermiLab)] {{doi-inline|10.1103/PhysRevLett.107.102001|(''Phys. Rev. Lett.'')}}
** The world's most powerful "[[split magnet]]" – one that is made in two halves with holes in the middle to observe experiments – has been built in the US. It operates at 25 [[Tesla (unit)|Tesla]], equivalent to 500,000 times the strength of Earth's [[geomagnetic field|magnetic field]]. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/technology-14204608 (BBC)]
* 21 July
** [[Space Shuttle Atlantis|Space Shuttle ''Atlantis'']] completes [[STS-135]], the 135th and final mission of the [[Space Shuttle program]]. [http://www.spaceflightnow.com/shuttle/sts135/ (Spaceflight Now)]
** A 120-million-year-old fossil is the oldest pregnant [[lizard]] ever discovered, according to scientists. The fossil, found in [[China]], is a very complete 30-cm (12-in)-long specimen with more than a dozen embryos in its body. [https://web.archive.org/web/20110721213807/http://www.bbc.co.uk/nature/14200255 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1007/s00114-011-0820-1|(''Naturwissenschaften'')}}
** Researchers at the [[University of Minnesota]] have discovered a [[gene]] required to maintain male gender throughout life. [http://medicalxpress.com/news/2011-07-gene-required-male-sex-life.html (Medical Xpress)] {{doi-inline|10.1038/nature10239|(''Nature'')}}
[[File:STS-135 landing cropped.jpg|thumb|right|21 July 2011: the final mission of the [[Space Shuttle program]], [[STS-135]], ends with the landing of [[Space Shuttle Atlantis|Space Shuttle ''Atlantis'']] (pictured) at [[Kennedy Space Center]].]]
* 26 July
** Using [[silicon]] [[lithography]], liquid [[silicone]], and [[electrodes]] that are fashioned into patterns invisible to the naked eye, researchers at [[Stanford University]] have created transparent electric [[battery (electricity)|batteries]]. [http://www.technologyreview.com/energy/38132/ (''Technology Review'')] {{doi-inline|10.1073/pnas.1102873108|(''PNAS'')}}
** [[DNA]] circuits have been used to make a neural network and to store memories. [https://arstechnica.com/science/news/2011/07/artificial-neural-network-used-for-memory-created-using-dna-computing.ars (Ars Technica)] {{doi-inline|10.1038/nature10262|(''Nature'')}}
* 28 July – A [[China|Chinese]] fossil of a previously unknown [[bird]]-like dinosaur is estimated by scientists to be about 155 million years old – five million years older than [[Archaeopteryx]], which for 150 years has been assumed to be the world's earliest bird. [https://web.archive.org/web/20110801143653/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/science/dinosaurs/8666077/Newly-discovered-dinosaur-could-disprove-earliest-bird-theory.html (''Daily Telegraph'')] {{doi-inline|10.1038/nature10288|(''Nature'')}}
* 29 July – A major clinical trial will investigate whether [[stem cell]]s can be safely used to stop or even reverse the damage caused by [[multiple sclerosis]]. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/health-14332206 (BBC)]

=== August ===
* 3 August – Researchers suggest that [[Earth]] once had a small second [[Moon]] that was destroyed in a slow-motion collision with the far side of its larger companion. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14391929 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1038/nature10289|(''Nature'')}}
* 4 August
** New images from the [[Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter]] appear to show evidence of flowing, [[liquid water]] on [[Mars]]. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14408928 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1126/science.1204816|(''Science'')}}
** A ring of [[antiprotons]] is detected around the Earth. [https://www.newscientist.com/article/mg21128245.500-antiproton-ring-found-around-earth.html (New Scientist)] [http://nextbigfuture.com/2011/08/confirmation-of-geomagnetically-trapped.html (Next Big Future)] {{doi-inline|10.1088/2041-8205/737/2/L29|(''Astrophys. J. Lett.'')}}
** Artificial [[sperm]] are created using [[stem cell]]s for the first time, in a scientific breakthrough that could lead to new treatments for infertile men. [https://web.archive.org/web/20110805012134/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/science/science-news/8682142/Stem-cells-used-to-make-artificial-sperm.html (''Daily Telegraph'')] {{doi-inline|10.1016/j.cell.2011.06.052|(''Cell'')}}
* 5 August
** The solar-powered probe ''[[Juno (spacecraft)|Juno]]'' is launched from [[Kennedy Space Center]] on a five-year mission to [[Jupiter]]. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14412988 (BBC)] [http://www.nasa.gov/mission_pages/juno/news/juno20110805.html (NASA)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200426125459/https://www.nasa.gov/mission_pages/juno/news/juno20110805.html |date=2020-04-26 }}
** Bypassing [[stem cell]]s, scientists have made [[neurons]] directly from [[human skin]]. {{doi-inline|10.1016/j.cell.2011.07.007|(''Cell'')}}
** Scientists have developed a new class of molecules that target cells' entry systems to ensure harmful organisms do not gain access. The molecules, nicknamed pitstops, could lead to new therapeutic approaches to prevent the spread of viral and bacterial infections. [http://www.abc.net.au/science/articles/2011/08/05/3285707.htm (ABC Science)] {{doi-inline|10.1016/j.cell.2011.06.025|(''Cell'')}}
* 6 August – A study postulates that the demise of the world's forests [[Permian-Triassic extinction event|250 million years ago]] was likely accelerated by aggressive tree-killing [[fungi]], who flourished in conditions brought about by global [[climate change]].  {{doi-inline|10.1130/G32178.1|(''Geology'')}}
[[File:Chronic lymphocytic leukemia.jpg|thumb|right|10 August 2011: a new [[gene therapy]] successfully kills off [[leukemia]] [[lymphocytes]] (pictured) in three advanced patients.]]
* 8 August – A report, based on [[NASA]] analysis of [[meteorites]] found on [[Earth]], suggests that the building blocks of [[DNA]] ([[adenine]], [[guanine]] and related [[organic molecules]]) may have been formed in [[outer space]].  {{doi-inline|10.1073/pnas.1106493108|(''PNAS'')}}
* 10 August – A new [[gene therapy]] that has successfully neutralized advanced cases of [[chronic lymphocytic leukemia]] (CLL) in 3 patients is published. [https://www.foxnews.com/health// (Fox News)] {{doi-inline|10.1056/NEJMoa1103849|(''NEJM'')}} [http://www.scientificamerican.com/article.cfm?id=reprogrammed-cells-cure-leukemia (''Scientific American'')]
* 11 August
** Researchers say they have created the first-ever animal with artificial information in its genetic code. The technique, they say, could give biologists "atom-by-atom control" over the molecules in living organisms. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14492948 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1021/ja2054034|(''J. Am. Chem. Soc.'')}}
** [[Arctic ice]] might be thinning four times faster than predicted by the [[IPCC]], according to a new study by [[MIT]]'s Department of Earth, Atmosphere, and Planetary Sciences (EAPS). [http://web.mit.edu/newsoffice/2011/arctic-ice-melt-0810.html (MIT)] {{doi-inline|10.1029/2011JC007110|(''J. Geophys. Res.'')}}
** Scientists have shown how an [[enzyme]] from a [[microbe]] can quickly and cheaply produce [[hydrogen]] from water. Hydrogen is seen as vital to future energy systems, but its production has previously been too costly and time-consuming to be viable on a large scale. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14494972 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1126/science.1205864|(''Science'')}}
* 12 August – An ultra-thin, flexible electronic circuit that can be stuck to the skin like a temporary [[tattoo]] is developed, with possible applications in [[cellphone]] and [[mobile computing]] technology. [https://www.independent.co.uk/news/science/how-computers-will-soon-get-under-our-skin-2336246.html (''The Independent'')] {{doi-inline|10.1126/science.1206157|(''Science'')}}
* 16 August
** Private donors, including actress [[Jodie Foster]], raise enough money to re-open the mothballed [[SETI]] radio telescope array, allowing SETI to continue its search for extraterrestrial intelligence. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14544953 (BBC)]
** A study of fossilised plants suggests that [[wood]]y plants first appeared on the Earth about 10 million years earlier than previously thought. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14543120 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1126/science.1208882|(''Science'')}}
** [[Taiwan]]ese researchers report that 15 minutes of exercise a day can boost [[life expectancy]] by three years and cut death risk by 14%. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/health-14526853 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1016/S0140-6736(11)60749-6|(''The Lancet'')}}
* 17 August
** [[DARPA]] is offering $500,000 to study what it would take—organizationally, technically, sociologically and ethically—to [[interstellar travel|send humans to another star]], a challenge of such magnitude that the study alone could take a hundred years. [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/08/18/science/space/18starship.html (''The New York Times'')]
** Researchers at the [[University of Edinburgh]] state that [[near-death experience]]s are the work of neural pathway disturbances caused by a disruption of the [[oxygen]] supply to the brain, and are not [[supernatural]] events. [http://www.scientificamerican.com/article.cfm?id=peace-of-mind-near-death (''Scientific American'')] {{doi-inline|10.1016/j.tics.2011.07.010|(''Trends Cogn. Sci.'')}}
* 18 August
** [[IBM]] has developed a microprocessor which it claims comes closer than ever to replicating the human brain. The system is capable of "rewiring" its connections as it encounters new information, similar to the way biological synapses work. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/technology-14574747 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1145/1978542.1978559|''Comm. ACM''}}
** Within decades, [[solar flare|solar storms]] are likely to become more disruptive to planes and spacecraft, say researchers at [[Reading University]]. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14580995 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1029/2011GL048489|(''Geophys. Res. Lett.'')}}
[[File:Explosions.jpg|thumb|right|19 August 2011: the American [[Office of Naval Research]] successfully tests a new class of conventional explosive, reportedly five times more powerful than existing explosives.]]
* 19 August – The US [[Office of Naval Research]] says that it has successfully tested a new type of explosive material that can dramatically increase weapons' impacts. Missiles made from the high-density substance can explode with up to five times the energy of existing explosives. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14581097 (BBC)]
* 22 August – American researchers prototype a basic form of bulletproof skin, based on genetically modified [[silkworm]] threads. [http://www.policeone.com/police-products/body-armor/articles/4241091-Researchers-create-bulletproof-skin/ (Police One)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111023193751/http://www.policeone.com/police-products/body-armor/articles/4241091-Researchers-create-bulletproof-skin/ |date=2011-10-23 }}
* 23 August
** The natural world contains about 8.7 million [[species]], according to a new estimate described by scientists as the most accurate ever. However, the vast majority of these species have not been identified – cataloguing them all could take more than 1,000 years. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14616161 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1371/journal.pbio.1001127|(''PLoS Biol.'')}}
** Computer simulations suggest that violent [[asteroid]] impacts flinging life from Earth to other planets is more likely than previously thought. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14637109 (BBC)] [https://arxiv.org/abs/1108.3375 (ArXiv)]
* 24 August – [[Antibiotics]]' impact on [[gut bacteria]] is permanent—and so serious in its long-term consequences that medicine should consider whether to restrict the prescription of antibiotics to pregnant women and young children, according to a new study. [https://www.wired.com/wiredscience/2011/08/killing-beneficial-bacteria/ (Wired)] {{doi-inline|10.1038/476393a|(''Nature'')}}
* 25 August – A [[monkey]] sporting a ginger beard and matching fiery red tail, discovered in a threatened region of the [[Brazil]]ian [[Amazon Rainforest|Amazon]], is believed to be a species new to science. [https://www.theguardian.com/environment/2011/aug/25/new-monkey-species-amazon (''The Guardian'')]
* 26 August – An [[atomic clock]] at the UK's [[National Physical Laboratory (United Kingdom)|National Physical Laboratory]] (NPL) has the best long-term accuracy of any clock in the world, researchers from NPL and [[Penn State University]] have found. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14657002 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1088/0026-1394/48/5/007|(''Metrologia'')}}
* 29 August – Japanese scientists announce an innovation in [[wind turbine]] technology, the [[wind lens]], which could triple the energy output of wind turbines, making wind energy affectively cheaper than nuclear energy. [http://www.mnn.com/green-tech/research-innovations/blogs/japanese-breakthrough-will-make-wind-power-cheaper-than-nuclea (Mother Nature Network)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111031021912/http://www.mnn.com/green-tech/research-innovations/blogs/japanese-breakthrough-will-make-wind-power-cheaper-than-nuclea |date=2011-10-31 }} [http://www.riam.kyushu-u.ac.jp/windeng/en_aboutus_detail04.html (Kyushu University)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120101010531/http://www.riam.kyushu-u.ac.jp/windeng/en_aboutus_detail04.html |date=2012-01-01 }}
* 31 August
** An engineered [[virus]], injected into the blood, can selectively target and destroy [[cancer]] cells throughout the body, in what researchers have labelled a medical first. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/health-14730608 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1038/nature10358|(''Nature'')}}
** A pill to prevent [[sunburn]] is being developed, using [[coral]]'s natural defence against the sun's harmful [[ultraviolet]] rays. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/health-14717834 (BBC)]
** [[Graphene]], the strongest known material on Earth, could help boost [[broadband]] internet speed, say researchers. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/technology-14730689 (BBC)]
** [[Advanced Micro Devices|AMD]] has broken the world [[overclocking]] speed record, thanks to the use of [[liquid nitrogen]] and [[liquid helium]] [[computer cooling|coolant]]. The company achieved an overclocked frequency of 8.429 [[GHz#Computing|GHz]] on a near-production, eight-core [[Bulldozer (processor)|AMD FX 8150 Bulldozer]] processor sample. [https://archive.today/20130420102346/http://www.eweekeurope.co.uk/news/supercooled-amd-bulldozer-breaks-overclocking-record-39553 (eweek europe)]

=== September ===
[[File:Nanobrain.tif|thumb|right|2 September 2011: scientists create a working electric motor made from [[Single molecule electric motor|a single molecule]] (molecular-scale computer pictured).]]
* 2 September
** Researchers create the [[Single molecule electric motor|smallest electric motor]] yet devised, made from a single [[molecule]] around a [[nanometre]] across. The invention could have applications in both [[nanotechnology]] and medicine. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14763223 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1038/nnano.2011.142|(''Nat. Nanotechnol.'')}}
** Researchers report two major breakthroughs in [[quantum computing]]—a quantum system built on the familiar [[von Neumann architecture|von Neumann processor-memory architecture]], and a working digital quantum simulator built on a quantum-computer platform. [http://www.popsci.com/technology/article/2011-09/two-key-computing-advances-bring-quantum-computers-closer-reality (PopSci)] {{doi-inline|10.1126/science.1208001|(''Science''-1)}} {{doi-inline|10.1126/science.1208517|(''Science''-2)}}
** [[Yale University]] researchers have discovered the source of signals that trigger [[hair]] growth, an insight that may lead to new treatments for [[baldness]]. {{doi-inline|10.1016/j.cell.2011.07.019|(''Cell'')}}
** Scientists map the taste [[Cerebral cortex|cortex]] in [[mice]], pinpointing the brain regions that detect certain flavors. [http://www.popsci.com/science/article/2011-09/scientists-map-mouse-taste-cortex-pinpointing-brain-regions-detect-certain-flavors (PopSci)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110910152307/http://www.popsci.com/science/article/2011-09/scientists-map-mouse-taste-cortex-pinpointing-brain-regions-detect-certain-flavors |date=2011-09-10 }} {{doi-inline|10.1126/science.1204076|(''Science'')}}
** Researchers suggest that dry desert planets might be the most common type of habitable planet in the galaxy, rather than watery planets such as [[Earth]]. {{doi-inline|10.1089/ast.2010.0545|(''Astrobiology'')}}
** [[Challacombe scale]] for a clinical oral dryness score introduced. {{doi-inline|10.1038/sj.bdj.2011.884|(''Br. Dent. J.'')}}
* 8 September
** [[Cuba]]n medical authorities release [[CimaVax-EGF]], the first therapeutic [[vaccine]] for [[lung cancer]]. The vaccine was the result of a 25-year research project at [[Havana]]'s [[Center of Molecular Immunology]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20111011071402/http://news.xinhuanet.com/english2010/health/2011-09/07/c_131111574.htm (Xinhua)]
** [[University of Glasgow]] scientists have taken their first tentative steps towards creating "life" from inorganic chemical cells, potentially defining the new area of "inorganic biology." [https://www.newscientist.com/article/dn20906-lifelike-cells-are-made-of-metal.html (''New Scientist'')] {{doi-inline|10.1002/anie.201105068|(''Angew. Chem.'')}}
* 9 September – Feeding a [[supercomputer]] with news stories could help predict major world events, according to US researchers. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/technology-14841018 (BBC)] [http://www.uic.edu/htbin/cgiwrap/bin/ojs/index.php/fm/article/viewArticle/3663/3040 (''First Monday'')] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130411092238/http://www.uic.edu/htbin/cgiwrap/bin/ojs/index.php/fm/article/viewArticle/3663/3040 |date=2013-04-11 }}
* 12 September
** [[Arctic sea ice]] has melted to a historic low, researchers from the [[University of Bremen]] in [[Germany]] report. [https://web.archive.org/web/20110929100654/http://news.blogs.cnn.com/2011/09/12/arctic-ice-levels-hit-historic-low-researchers-say/ (CNN)] [http://www.iup.uni-bremen.de:8084/amsr/minimum2011-en.pdf (AMSR-E)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120118215823/http://www.iup.uni-bremen.de:8084/amsr/minimum2011-en.pdf |date=2012-01-18 }}
** Astronomers using the [[European Southern Observatory]]'s [[HARPS]] instrument announce the discovery of more than 50 new [[exoplanets]] – including 16 [[super-Earths]] – with one planet reportedly orbiting at the edge of the [[Goldilocks zone|habitable zone]] of its star. By studying the properties of all the HARPS planets found so far, the team has found that about 40% of stars similar to the [[Sun]] have at least one planet lighter than [[Saturn]]. [http://www.eso.org/public/news/eso1134/ (ESO)]
[[File:Art of SLS launch.jpg|thumb|right|14 September 2011: [[NASA]] publishes the design of its future heavy-lift rocket, the [[Space Launch System]] (concept art shown).]]
* 13 September
** The relative risks to the supply of some of Earth's [[rare-earth element|rarest elements]] have been detailed in a new list published by the [[British Geological Survey]] (BGS). [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14903904 (BBC)] [http://www.bgs.ac.uk/mineralsuk/statistics/riskList.html (BGS)]
** Researchers have developed a sophisticated camera system able to [[lie detector|detect lies]] by watching facial movements during speech. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14900800 (BBC)]
* 14 September
** [[NASA]] unveils the design for [[Space Launch System|a new heavy-lift rocket]] to take humans to [[Mars]] and the [[asteroids]]. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14915725 (BBC)]
** Researchers may have discovered how to safely open and close the [[blood–brain barrier]], offering a new way to safely deliver therapies to treat [[Alzheimer's disease]], [[multiple sclerosis]] and cancers of the central nervous system directly to the brain. [http://medicalxpress.com/news/2011-09-breaching-blood-brain-barrier-year-old-puzzle.html (Medical Xpress)] {{doi-inline|10.1523/JNEUROSCI.3337-11.2011|(''J. Neurosci.'')}}
* 15 September – A piece of [[amber]] discovered in [[Alberta]], Canada, contains an 80-million-year-old [[feather]] that could provide clues to the [[Origin of birds|relationship]] between [[dinosaurs]] and modern avian species. [https://www.cbc.ca/news/science/dinosaur-feathers-found-in-alberta-amber-1.1086765 (CBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1126/science.1212049|(''Science'')}}
* 16 September
** Scientists' predictions about the formation and characteristics of [[dark matter]] have been shaken by research into [[dwarf galaxies]] surrounding the [[Milky Way]]. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14948730 (BBC)]
** Artificial [[blood vessels]] made on a [[3D printer]] may soon be used for transplants of lab-created organs. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/technology-14946808 (BBC)]
* 19 September – People with [[schizophrenia]] are six times more likely to develop [[epilepsy]], reports a [[Taiwan]]ese study, which found a strong relationship between the two diseases. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/health-14948264 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1111/j.1528-1167.2011.03268.x|(''Epilepsia'')}}
* 20 September – US researchers say they have demonstrated how [[fuel cell]]s powered by [[bacteria]] can be "self-powered" and produce a limitless supply of [[hydrogen]] for [[hydrogen car]]s. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-14976893 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1073/pnas.1106335108|(''PNAS'')}}
* 22 September
** An international team of scientists at [[CERN]] records [[neutrino]] particles apparently traveling faster than the [[speed of light]]. If confirmed, the discovery would overturn [[Albert Einstein]]'s 1905 [[special theory of relativity]], which says that nothing can travel faster than light. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-15017484 (BBC)] [https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.4897 (ArXiv)]
** A non-disease-causing [[virus]] kills human [[breast cancer]] cells in the laboratory, creating opportunities for potential new cancer therapies, according to [[Penn State College of Medicine]] researchers who tested the virus on three different breast cancer types. [https://web.archive.org/web/20110924054651/http://live.psu.edu/story/55260 (Pennsylvania State University)]
* 24 September – [[NASA]]'s [[Upper Atmosphere Research Satellite]] (UARS) deorbits and impacts the [[Pacific Ocean]], having been decommissioned in 2005. UARS, which was launched in 1991, was designed for the study of [[Earth's atmosphere]], particularly the [[ozone layer]]. [http://www.nasa.gov/mission_pages/uars/index.html (NASA)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180225204106/http://www.nasa.gov/mission_pages/uars/index.html |date=2018-02-25 }}
* 26 September – Researchers have demonstrated that [[electron]]s can move freely in layers of linked [[semiconductor]] [[nanoparticles]] under the influence of light. This discovery may assist the development of cheap and efficient [[quantum dot]] [[solar cell]]s. {{doi-inline|10.1038/nnano.2011.159|(''Nat. Nanotechnol.'')}}
* 27 September
** Scientists have successfully replaced an injured part of a [[rat]]'s brain with a synthetic substitute. [https://www.newscientist.com/article/mg21128315.700-rat-cyborg-gets-digital-cerebellum.html (''New Scientist'')] [https://web.archive.org/web/20120117040430/http://www.sens.org/node/2210 (SENS Foundation)]
** Scientists have created a nanostructure which can multiply [[stem cells]] used in therapies – a first step towards developing large-scale stem cell culture factories. [http://www.labmate-online.com/news/news-and-views/5/university_of_southampton/stem_cell_breakthrough_has_therapies_potential/16830/ (Labmate online)] {{doi-inline|10.1038/nmat3058|(''Nat. Mater.'')}}
* 29 September
** A rocket carrying [[China]]'s first space laboratory module, [[Tiangong-1]], is successfully launched, marking the start of the [[Tiangong program]] to create a modular [[space station]]. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-15112760 (BBC)]
** A new method for [[self-healing material]]s is presented, inspired by mammalian [[vasculature]]. [https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-15096393 (BBC)] {{doi-inline|10.1098/rsif.2011.0508|(''J. R. Soc. Interface'')}}
** [[Geothermal power plants]] could help produce [[lithium]] for [[electric cars]], by way of a new process which extracts lithium from the [[brine]]s used to generate electricity in a geothermal power plant. [http://www.scientificamerican.com/article.cfm?id=geothermal-power-plants-could-help-produce-lithium-for-electric-cars (''Scientific American'')]
* 30 September
** Scientists release the most accurate simulation of the structure of the [[universe]] to date. [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gmcvFmlkYjY (YouTube)] {{doi-inline|10.1088/0004-637X/740/2/102|(''Astrophys. J.'')}}
